creator: yetoohappy@gmail.com
text:
(In reply to Warner Losh from comment #2)
To be clear the Freebsd/Fedora instance was never put to sleep it was always the Windows or the Debian machine that was put into S3 sleep. The problem and testing wasn't for Freebsd/Fedora instances being pingable while Windows/Debian was in S3 sleep (or vice versa). It was after Windows woke up from S3 sleep that it couldn't ping Freebsd. I will try the instructions to load the USB quirk.
